sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Fund Phase 1 of 2 to take Bifrost from working testnet to launch readiness, including hardening, security audits, formal verification, ecosystem and partner readiness, stewardship, and economic foundations for launch.

Deliverables: Audited Bitcoin-Cardano bridge running on Cardano mainnet in federated and SPO-threshold custody modes under controlled access, Security audits and formal verification, Hardened economic model, Independent stewardship structure or equivalent form determined in Phase 1, SPO and dApp partner pipeline required for public launch, Public reporting of operations, milestone progress, treasury disbursements, and on-chain activity
Deadline/expiry: 9-month delivery period from July 2026 to March 2027

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's existing SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as native fBTC for DeFi use cases.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The requested treasury withdrawal is ₳12,332,031, approximately $1,973,125 at 0.16 USD/ADA, including a 10% refundable contingency.

Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): Bifrost is currently funded through Catalyst Fund 14 for ₳739,000 and is intended to prove the architecture end-to-end on testnet by August 2026.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that by the end of Phase 1 Bifrost will be audited and running on Cardano mainnet in both federated and SPO-threshold custody modes under controlled access.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Bifrost custody of locked BTC is described as distributed across 400+ Cardano SPOs, weighted by delegation, with fund movements requiring a cryptographic threshold representing the majority of delegated stake.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal states that fBTC will be issued and burned 1:1 with BTC locked in the Bifrost custody script on Bitcoin.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal states that by the end of Phase 1 Bifrost will be audited and running on Cardano mainnet in both federated and SPO-threshold custody modes under controlled access.

Independent evidence for: The proposer claims Bifrost will be public infrastructure with no bridge token, no founder allocation, Apache 2.0 open source code, public accountability, and independent stewardship.
Independent evidence for: The proposal targets 1,200 BTC, about 600,000 annual transactions, and 3,000 fBTC-holding wallets by Q2 2029 as its base contribution to Cardano 2030 KPIs.
Independent evidence for: The proposal claims bridge fees in fBTC will fund operations and that surplus will be split to SPOs and the Cardano Treasury.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's existing SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as native fBTC for DeFi use cases." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal states that by the end of Phase 1 Bifrost will be audited and running on Cardano mainnet in both federated and SPO-threshold custody modes under controlled access." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
